name: threefive3

metadata.name: threefive3
classifiers: Operating System :: Microsoft :: Windows :: Windows 3.1 or Earlier
metadata.summary: threefive3 is a SCTE-35 Decoder and Encoder for MPEGTS, HLS and Everything else.

documentation:
# `scte35`
threefive continued.  SCTE-35 for the People.
# Now with Super Advanced Error Detection Stuff. 
* Does not generate Fatal errors, it won't break your process.
* Displays what is in error and how to correct it.
* Works in the cli
* Works in code.
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/581e0081-0c9d-4f9c-87a2-dd7f4cf3ce8c)





# `Install`
* python3 via pip
```rebol
python3 -mpip install scte35
```
* pypy3 
```rebol
pypy3 -mpip install scte35
```
* from the git repo
```rebol
git clone https://github.com/superkabuki/scte35.git
cd scte35
make install
```
___


# `The Cli tool`
> The cli audetects data being available on stdin and that allows it to autodetect the SCTE-35 format as well as MPEGTS steams. Now every SCTE-35 data format, except hls, is autodetected on the commandline, as well as stdin.HLS requires the `hls` ketyword.

### The cli tool installs automatically with pip or the Makefile.

* [__SCTE-35 Inputs__](#inputs)
* [__SCTE-35 Outputs__](#outputs)
* [__Parse __MPEGTS__ streams for __SCTE-35__](#streams)
* [Parse __SCTE-35__ in __hls__](#hls)
* [Display __MPEGTS__ __iframes__](#iframes)
* [Display raw __SCTE-35 packets__ from __video streams__](#packets)
* [__Repair SCTE-35 streams__ changed to __bin data__ by __ffmpeg__](#sixfix)


### `Inputs` 
* the cli can __decode SCTE-35__ from
 * [__Base64__](#base64)
 * [__Hex,__](#hex)
 * [__HLS,__](#hls)
 * [__JSON,__](#json)
 * [__Xml,__](#xml)
 * [__Xml+Bin__](#xmlbin)
 * [__MPEGTS Streams__](#streams)

* Most __inputs__ are __auto-detected.__ 
* __stdin__ is __auto selected__ and __auto detected.__
* __SCTE-35 data is printed to stderr__
* __stdout is used when piping video__
#### `Base64` 
* parse SCTE-35 encoded in Base64
```rebol
scte35 '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU='
```

#### `Hex`
* parse SCTE-35 encoded in Hex
```rebol
scte35 0xfc302c00000003289800fff00a05000000017f5f999901010011020f43554549000000007f8001003500002d974195
```

#### `HLS`

* parse SCTE-35 from HLS manifests and segments
```rebol
scte35 hls https://example.com/master.m3u8
```

#### `Json`

```rebol
cat json.json | scte35
```
#### `Xml`
* you can make a xml.xml file like this:
  * redirect 2 (stderr) to the file 
```awk
./scte352  '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU=' xml 2> xml.xml
```
* pass in
```rebol
scte35  < xml.xml
```
#### `Xmlbin`

```rebol
scte35 < xmlbin.xml
```
___
### `Outputs`

 * the cli can __encode SCTE-35__ to
 * [__Base64__](#base64-1)
 * [__Bytes__](#bytes)
 * [__Hex,__](#hex-1)
 * [__HLS,__](#hls-1)
 * [__JSON,__](#json-1)
 * [__Xml,__](#xml-1)
 * [__Xml+Bin__](#xmlbin-1)

* default output is `json`

#### json
```rebol
scte35 '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU='
```

#### `base64`
```rebol
scte35 '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU=' base64
```
#### `bytes`
```rebol
scte35 '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU=' bytes
```
#### `hex`
```rebol
scte35 '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU=' hex
```
#### `int`
```rebol
scte35 '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU=' int
```
#### `xml`
```rebol
scte35 '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU=' xml
```
#### `xml+bin`
```rebol
scte35 '/DAsAAAAAyiYAP/wCgUAAAABf1+ZmQEBABECD0NVRUkAAAAAf4ABADUAAC2XQZU=' xmlbin
```
___
## Streams
### `File and Network Protocols`

#### `File` 
```rebol
scte35 video.ts
```
#### `Http(s)` 
```rebol
scte35 https://example.com/master.m3u8
```
#### `Multicast`
```rebol
scte35 udp://@235.35.3.5:9999
```
#### `stdin`
```rebol
cat video.ts | scte35
```
#### `Udp Unicast`
```rebol
scte35 udp://10.0.0.7:5555
```
___

### `hls`
* parse hls manifests and segments for SCTE-35
```rebol
scte35 hls https://example.com/master.m3u8
```
___
### `Iframes`
* Show iframes PTS in an MPEGTS video
```rebol
scte35 iframes https://example.com/video.ts
```
___
### `packets`   
* Print raw SCTE-35 packets from multicast mpegts video
```rebol
scte35 packets udp://@235.35.3.5:3535
```
___
### `proxy`   
* Parse a https stream and write raw video to stdout
```rebol
scte35 proxy video.ts
```
___
### `pts`    
* Print PTS from mpegts video
```rebol
 scte35 pts video.ts
```
___
### `sidecar`  
* Parse a stream, write pts,write SCTE-35 Cues to sidecar.txt
```rebol
  scte35 sidecar video.ts
```
___
### `sixfix`  
* Fix SCTE-35 data mangled by ffmpeg
```rebol
 scte35 sixfix video.ts
```
___
### `show`  

* Probe mpegts video _( kind of like ffprobe )_
```rebol
 scte35 show video.ts
```
___
### `version`     
* Show version
```rebol
 scte35 version
```
___
### `help`        
* Help
```rebol
 scte35 help
```
___

methods.name: scte35.segment.Segment.decode
documentation: decode a mpegts segment.

The Segment class is a Sub Class of scte35.Stream
made for small, fixed size MPEGTS files,like HLS segments.

Segment Class Specific Features:

* Decryption of AES Encrypted MPEGTS.

* Segment.cues  a list of SCTE35 cues found in the segment.


Example:

    from scte35 import Segment

    >>>> uri = "https://example.com/1.ts"
    >>>> seg = Segment(uri)
    >>>> seg.decode()
    >>>> [cue.encode() for cue in seg.cues]
    ['/DARAAAAAAAAAP/wAAAAAHpPv/8=',
    '/DAvAAAAAAAAAP/wFAUAAAKWf+//4WoauH4BTFYgAAEAAAAKAAhDVUVJAAAAAOv1oqc=']


AES Encryption Example:

    from scte35 import Segment

    >>>> key = "https://example.com/aes.key"
    >>>> IV=0x998C575D24F514AEC84EDC5CABCCDB81
    >>>> uri = "https://example.com/aes-1.ts"

    >>>> seg = Segment(uri,key_uri=key, iv=IV)
    >>>> seg.decode()
    >>>> {cue.packet_data.pts:cue.encode() for cue in seg.cues}

   { 89718.451333: '/DARAAAAAAAAAP/wAAAAAHpPv/8=',
   89730.281789: '/DAvAAAAAAAAAP/wFAUAAAKWf+//4WoauH4BTFYgAAEAAAAKAAhDVUVJAAAAAOv1oqc='}

methods.name: scte35.xml.Node.add_comment
documentation: add_comment add a Comment node

The Node class is to create an xml node.

An instance of Node has:

    name :      <name> </name>
    value  :    <name>value</name>
    attrs :     <name attrs[k]="attrs[v]">
    children :  <name><children[0]></children[0]</name>
    depth:      tab depth for printing (automatically set)

Use like this:

    from scte35.xml import Node

    ts = Node('TimeSignal')
    st = Node('SpliceTime',attrs={'pts_time':3442857000})
    ts.add_child(st)
    print(ts)

classes.name: scte35.xml.Comment
documentation:
The Comment class is to create a Node representing a xml comment.

An instance of Comment has:

    name :      <!-- name -->
    depth:      tab depth for printing (automatically set)

Since Comment is a Node, it also has attrs, value and children but
these are ignored. cf etree.Comment
Use like this:

    from scte35.xml import Comment, Node

    n = Node('root')
    c = Comment('my first comment')

    n.add_child(c)
    print(n)

See also Node.add_comment:

load_info_section loads data for Cue.info_section

load_command loads data for Cue.command

Load_descriptors loads descriptor data.

a descriptor instance will be created.

methods.name: scte35.cue.Cue._no_cmd
documentation: _no_cmd raises an exception if no splice command.

methods.name: scte35.cue.Cue.load
documentation:
Cue.load loads SCTE35 data for encoding.
gonzo is a dict or json
with any or all of these keys
gonzo = {
    'info_section': {dict} ,
    'command': {dict},
    'descriptors': [list of {dicts}],
    }

methods.name: scte35.cue.Cue.from_xml
documentation:
build_cue takes the data put into the gonzo dict
and builds a scte35.Cue instance

methods.name: scte35.cue.Cue._xml_mk_descriptor
documentation: _mk_descriptor_xml make xml nodes for descriptors.

methods.name: scte35.cue.Cue.xml
documentation:
xml returns a scte35.Node instance
which can be edited as needed or printed.
xmlbin

methods.name: scte35.cue.Cue.xmlbin
documentation:
xml returns a scte35.Node instance
which can be edited as needed or printed.
xmlbin

The scte35.Splice class handles parsing
SCTE 35 message strings.
Example usage:

>>>> import fu
>>>> Base64 = "/DAvAAAAAAAA///wBQb+dGKQoAAZAhdDVUVJSAAAjn+fCAgAAAAALKChijUCAKnMZ1g="
>>>> cue = scte35.Cue(Base64)

# cue.decode() returns True on success,or False if decoding failed

>>>> cue.decode()
True

# After Calling cue.decode() the instance variables can be accessed via dot notation.

>>>> cue.command
{'command_length': 5, 'name': 'Time Signal', 'time_specified_flag': True,
'pts_time': 21695.740089}

>>>> cue.command.pts_time
21695.740089

>>>> cue.info_section.table_id

'0xfc'

modules.name: scte35.bitn
documentation: The bitn.BitBin and bitn.NBin classes

classes.name: scte35.bitn.BitBin

methods.name: scte35.bitn.BitBin.as_90k
documentation:
Returns num_bits
of bits as 90k time

methods.name: scte35.bitn.BitBin.as_int
documentation:
Starting at self.idx of self.bits,
slice off num_bits of bits.

methods.name: scte35.bitn.BitBin.as_hex
documentation:
Returns the hex value
of num_bits of bits

methods.name: scte35.bitn.BitBin.as_charset
documentation:
Returns num_bits of bits
as bytes decoded as charset
default charset is ascii.

methods.name: scte35.bitn.BitBin.as_bytes
documentation:
Returns num_bits of bits
as bytes

methods.name: scte35.bitn.BitBin.as_flag
documentation: Returns one bit as True or False

methods.name: scte35.bitn.BitBin.forward
documentation:
Advances the start point
forward by num_bits

methods.name: scte35.bitn.BitBin.negative_shift
documentation:
negative_shift is called instead of
throwing a negative shift count error.

bitn.Bitbin takes a byte string and
converts it to a integer, a very large integer
if needed. A 1500 bit integer is no problem.
several methods are available for slicing off bits.

classes.name: scte35.bitn.NBin

methods.name: scte35.bitn.NBin.nbits2bites
documentation:
nbits2bites converts
the int self.nbits to bytes as self.bites
and sets self.nbits  and self.idx to 0

methods.name: scte35.bitn.NBin.add_bites
documentation:
add_bites appends plus_bites
to self.bites

methods.name: scte35.bitn.NBin.add_int
documentation: left shift nbits and append new_bits
if self.idx % 8 == 0:
self.nbits2bites()

methods.name: scte35.bitn.NBin.add_90k
documentation:
Converts 90k  float timestamps
to an int and appends it to nbits
via self.add_int

methods.name: scte35.bitn.NBin.add_hex
documentation:
add_hex converts a
hex encoded string to an int
and appends it to self.nbits
via self.add_int

methods.name: scte35.bitn.NBin.add_flag
documentation:
add_flag takes a boolean
value and adds it as an integer
to self.nbits via self.add_int

methods.name: scte35.bitn.NBin.reserve
documentation:
reserve sets 'num'  bits to 1
and appends them to self.nbits
via self.add_int

methods.name: scte35.bitn.NBin.forward
documentation: Currently just an alias to reserve

methods.name: scte35.bitn.NBin.zeroed
documentation: zeroed sets num bits to zero

bitn.NBin is
the reverse BitBin.
Encodes data to integers
and then bytes

documentation:
tsdata is an file or http/https url
set show_null=False to exclude Splice Nulls

Use like...

from scte35 import Stream
strm = Stream("vid.ts",show_null=False)
strm.decode()

documentation: parse a 13 bit pid value

documentation: parse a 16 bit program number value

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream._find_start
documentation:
_find_start locates the first SYNC_BYTE
parses 1 packet and returns True
if SYNC_BYTE is found.

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream.iter_pkts
documentation: iter_pkts iterates a mpegts stream into packets

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream.decode
documentation:
Stream.decode reads self.tsdata to find SCTE35 packets.
func can be set to a custom function that accepts
a scte35.Cue instance as it's only argument.

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream.decode_next
documentation:
Stream.decode_next returns the next
SCTE35 cue as a scte35.Cue instance.

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream.decode_program
documentation:
Stream.decode_program limits SCTE35 parsing
to a specific MPEGTS program.

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream.decode_pids
documentation:
Stream.decode_pids takes a list of SCTE-35 Pids parse
and an optional call back function to run when a Cue is found.
if scte35_pids is not set, all scte35 pids will be parsed.

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream.decode_start_time
documentation: decode_start_time

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream.proxy
documentation:
Stream.decode_proxy writes all ts packets are written to stdout
for piping into another program like mplayer.
SCTE-35 cues are print2`ed to stderr.

displays streams that will be
parsed for SCTE-35.

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream._parse_scte35
documentation: parse a scte35 cue from one or more packets

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream._parse_sdt
documentation: _parse_sdt parses the SDT for program metadata

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream._parse_pat
documentation:
parse program association table
for program to pmt_pid mappings.

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream._parse_pmt
documentation: parse program maps for streams

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream._parse_program_streams
documentation:
parse the elementary streams
from a program

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream._parse_stream_type
documentation: extract stream pid and type

methods.name: scte35.stream.Stream._set_scte35_pids
documentation:
if stream_type is 0x06 or 0x86
add it to self._scte35_pids.
Stream class for parsing MPEG-TS data.

modules.name: scte35.sxp
documentation:
sxp.py
home of the SuperXmlParser class

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.mk_attrs
documentation:
mk_attrs parses the current node for attributes
and stores them in self.stuff[self.active]

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.parsed
documentation:
parsed returns tag, attrs and value
for an xml element

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.mk_tag
documentation:
mk_tag parse out the
next available xml tag from data

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.mk_element
documentation:
mk_element slices out the tag element
inclusive: <tag > stuff</tag> and <tag attr="1"/>

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.mk
documentation: parse parses an xml string for a SCTE-35 Cue.

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.gimme
documentation: gimme get all instances of tag from exemel and return a list of them.

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.gimme_one
documentation: gimme_one get the first instance of tag from exemel and return it.

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.spliceinfosection
documentation:
spliceinfosection parses exemel for info section data
and returns a loadable dict

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.command
documentation:
command parses exemel for a splice command
and returns a loadable dict

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.timesignal
documentation:
timesignal parses exemel for TimeSignal data
and creates a loadable dict for the Cue class.

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.privatecommand
documentation:
privatecommand parses exemel for PrivateCommand
data and creates a loadable dict for the Cue class.

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.spliceinsert
documentation:
spliceinsert parses exemel for SpliceInsert data
and creates a loadable dict for the Cue class.

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.splicetime
documentation:
splicetime parses xml from a splice command
to get pts_time, sets time_specified_flag to True

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.breakduration
documentation:
breakduration parses xml for break duration, break_auto_return
and sets duration_flag to True.

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.segmentationdescriptor
documentation: segmentationdescriptor creates a dict to be loaded.

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.upids
documentation: upids parses out upids from a splice descriptors xml

methods.name: scte35.sxp.SuperXmlParser.xml2cue
documentation:
xml2cue returns a base64 string for xmlbin
and a dict for xml
